summary.intent: Maintain the httpd-crate branch by merging main repeatedly, resolving conflicts, and addressing all greptile code review comments on the PR.
summary.outcome: All merges completed, 23+ conflicts resolved, greptile findings fixed across two PR review rounds, but git-filter-repo rewrote all commit SHAs causing PR #296 to close; a new squashed PR #465 was created.

repo: rustls crypto provider `install_default()` must be called in `prepare_gateway` (not `start_gateway`) so both the normal and embedded/swift-bridge startup paths get TLS initialized
browser_tool_for_warmup must be extracted from the PreparedGatewayCore destructure and threaded through BannerMeta to reach start_browser_warmup_after_listener
_proxy_shutdown_tx in PreparedGateway must be held in the struct — dropping it via `..` pattern kills the trusted-network proxy
/ws route alias must be registered alongside /ws/chat for node-add generated commands to work
Session event enrichment (label, model, preview, channel binding) was lost when gateway logic moved to httpd — needed to be ported explicitly
httpd crate required moltis-agents as an explicit Cargo.toml dependency to reference AgentTool type
web/src/lib.rs had duplicate route registrations introduced during the merge
node-host launchd plist generation was vulnerable to XML injection from user-controlled paths (bin, log, data_dir)
node-host systemd unit file had unquoted values susceptible to injection/splitting
nightly rustfmt enforces grouped use blocks (merging separate `use moltis_gateway` and `use moltis_httpd` into a single braced block)

code.finding: prepare_gateway is the correct place for rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider().install_default() — covers both start_gateway and prepare_gateway_embedded paths
PreparedGateway struct must hold proxy_shutdown_tx to keep the proxy alive; destructuring with .. silently dropped it
start_browser_warmup_after_listener signature was updated to accept browser_tool parameter; the field on PreparedGatewayCore needed pub visibility
Local MAX_LIFETIME constant was shadowed by module-level MAX_BROWSER_INSTANCE_LIFETIME; the local one was unused
Plist string values must be XML-escaped; systemd unit values must be quoted to prevent injection from user-controlled paths
Extra blank line before test function caused nightly rustfmt failure

Test file added in main under crates/gateway/tests/ needed to live in crates/httpd/tests/ on this branch; imports also needed updating from moltis_gateway to moltis_httpd for build_gateway_base and finalize_gateway_app

workflow: git-filter-repo removes the 'origin' remote as a safety measure — must re-add with `git remote add origin` before pushing
Force-pushing rewritten history (filter-repo rewrites all SHAs) causes GitHub to close open PRs; they cannot be reopened via `gh pr reopen` after history divergence
gh CLI requires `gh repo set-default` in worktrees that lack a configured default remote
When filter-repo rewrites the entire repo history, the branch loses its common ancestor with main; cleanest recovery is to create a new branch from origin/main and apply the diff as a squashed commit
release.yml had a pattern of redundant pre-version build steps in each platform job (4 jobs × 1 wasted build each)

friction: git-filter-repo silently removed origin remote, which was not anticipated
Force-push after filter-repo closed PR #296 — GitHub cannot reopen a PR whose branch history no longer shares ancestry with base
filter-repo rewrote the entire repo history (all 2027 commits), not just branch commits, making rebase onto origin/main impossible without squashing
gh CLI needed repo set-default in the worktree context before any PR operations worked
Greptile findings required 3 separate fix-and-push rounds across two PRs (#296 and #465)
moltis-agents missing from httpd Cargo.toml caused a compile error mid-fix that required discovering and patching the dependency
open_items: PR #465 is a single squashed commit — individual commit history from the httpd-crate branch was lost; original per-feature commits are gone
Old PR #296 is closed and cannot be recovered; reviewers and comments from that PR are orphaned
The XML-escaping fix for plist is a best-effort string replacement — a proper XML builder library would be more robust long-term
release.yml redundant build removal was done for 4 jobs but should be audited for any additional platform variants added in future
